title: kalmia polifolia subsp. occidentalis
intro: Kalmia polifolia subsp. occidentalis, also known as western mountain laurel, is a shrub that belongs to the heath family and is native to North America. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. Western mountain laurel has been found to have various health benefits, including antioxidant and antiviral effects, as well as the potential to lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health. The plant can be prepared into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used in herbal remedies, and it is also sometimes used in traditional folk medicine to treat a range of ailments.
